progress_summary: v1.4 PR 1 (ADEV-328 — pack registry architecture) shipped as #192 → 4cb0b09. v1.4 PR 2 (ADEV-329 — backfill 5 language packs) shipped as #203 → 22c45cd: typescript, python, ruby, go, rust each with pack.yml (testRunner/buildCmd/lintCmd) + langRef language.md (4.9–6.2 KB; total 27.8 KB across all 5, well under the 50 KB soft cap). gen-stack-packs reports `5 pack(s), 5 coverage flag(s)`; site/src/lib/flags/registry.ts emits skills.coverage.{typescript,python,ruby,go,rust}.enabled. Equivalence test suite tests/stack-packs-real.test.js (16 cases) locks in the canonical commands per pack, the absence of frameworks: [...] (those arrive in PRs 4-7), each langRef under 50KB, and byte-equivalence of the generated coverage-flags.json. Vitest: 153/153 (was 137; +16 for the new equivalence suite). Next: PR 3 (ADEV-330) — three integration points in one PR per the v1.4 plan + user-approved hybrid driver semantics: (a) deploy-ops dispatcher reads pack.yml at runtime to pick deploy path per language (small, lookup-only), (b) api-dev codegens per-language scaffold templates at build time (richer; matches api-dev surface), (c) stack-forge dispatcher gains a kind:mobile branch as placeholder dispatch logic — iOS pack lands in PR 6, but the kind:mobile dispatch code + tests can land now to keep PR 6's diff narrow.

progress_table.label: Scope + 8-PR plan approved (incl. iOS pivot, distribution model)
Linear tickets ADEV-327..337 created and aligned with current scope
PR 1 — Architecture: schema, gen-stack-packs, auto-flag wiring, parity validator (ADEV-328)
PR 2 — Backfill 5 existing stacks as language packs + equivalence fixtures (ADEV-329 / #203 / 22c45cd)
PR 3 — deploy-ops dispatcher (runtime) + api-dev pack-read (codegen) + kind:mobile branch (ADEV-330)
PR 4 — Modern web bulk: phoenix, remix, sveltekit, solid, bun, deno (ADEV-334)
PR 5 — Enterprise bulk: spring-java, dotnet-aspnet, spring-kotlin, laravel-php (ADEV-335)
PR 6 — Apple/iOS/Swift pack + app-store adapter, release-checklist dispatch (ADEV-336)
PR 6.5 — Mobile bulk: kotlin-android + flutter + react-native-expo + play-store (ADEV-343)
PR 7 — Hosting adapters: railway, netlify, heroku, aws-amplify (ADEV-337)
PR 8 — Site /coverage page, v1.4 pill, 5 skill version bumps, release (ADEV-331)

key_decisions: Theme: pack registry replaces hardcoded stack tables in stack-forge / api-dev / deploy-ops / app-architect / migration-ops.
Each pack is skills/stack-forge/packs/<id>/pack.yml + references/*.md; kind ∈ { server | mobile | platform-adapter }.
Coverage flags auto-generated as skills.coverage.<pack-id>.enabled (server + mobile only; adapters don't get a flag). Default = true if status:stable, else false.
Distribution: bundle-everything via existing make-skills-zip.sh. Flags filter what stack-forge OFFERS, not what's downloaded. Keeps regulated/air-gapped installs working.

All of mobile ships in v1.4.0. Original plan deferred Android/Flutter/RN to v1.5 pending release-ops handling Play Store + EAS — release-ops landed in v1.3 (ADEV-308 Done 2026-05-08), opening the gate. PR 6 lands iOS first to dogfood kind: mobile dispatch; PR 6.5 (ADEV-343) brings Android + Flutter + React Native.
kind: mobile dispatch renders deploymentRef as a release checklist instead of executing commands. Output starts with explicit 'checklist-driven, not automated' notice.
Cloudflare stays the default platform after PR 3's dispatcher refactor; CF user output unchanged. Contract tests guard regressions.
Backfill (PR 2) ships snapshot equivalence fixtures so the 5 existing stacks produce byte-identical output to v1.2.
Only 5 skills version-bump 1.2.0 → 1.4.0 (stack-forge, api-dev, deploy-ops, app-architect, migration-ops). The other ~13 stay on 1.2.0.
Future option (v1.6+): split into opchain-core.zip + opchain-extras.zip if pack count crosses ~100 or refs get heavy. Document in CONTRIBUTING.md as guidance for pack authors.
PRs 4-7 parallelizable after PR 3 lands. PR 8 must merge last (badge count depends on final pack count).
ADEV-333 created in error during scope shift; cancelled as duplicate of ADEV-332. ADEV-332 repurposed as a rolling v1.4.x patch tracker (no fixed queue).
user_preferences: Pulls scope INTO releases when it expands the value prop; comfortable with bigger releases when 8-PR sequencing is clear.
Asks sharp clarifying questions about user-visible mechanics (downloads vs. visibility, plugin pattern) before approving implementation.
Prefers 'show me the implementation plan' as a checkpoint before writing code.
Wants Linear kept current with the actual plan — re-aligns tickets when scope shifts.

next_actions: PR 3 (ADEV-330): three integration points in one PR per the v1.4 plan. (1) deploy-ops dispatcher — read pack.yml at runtime to pick the deploy path per language/framework. Small lookup, no codegen; loads from src/generated/coverage-flags.json + per-pack reads as needed. (2) api-dev pack-read — codegen per-language scaffold templates at build time from pack.yml. New script (e.g. scripts/gen-api-dev-adapters.mjs) emits typed scaffolding modules consumed by api-dev's Designer/Builder phases. (3) stack-forge dispatcher kind:mobile branch — add placeholder dispatch logic + tests for kind:mobile packs; renders deploymentRef as a release-checklist instead of executing commands. iOS pack lands in PR 6 but the kind:mobile dispatch code can land now to keep PR 6 narrow.

PR 3: driver semantics are HYBRID (user-approved 2026-05-11): deploy-ops runtime read (small lookup), api-dev build-time codegen (richer per-language scaffolds). This is the explicit shape of integration that gets locked in by the equivalence tests.

PR 3: add tests in tests/ for each new entrypoint — deploy-ops dispatcher snapshot per language, api-dev scaffold codegen snapshot per language, kind:mobile dispatcher snapshot (synthetic mobile pack fixture). Aim for vitest 153 → ~170+.

PR 3: run npm run prebuild end-to-end; expect gen-stack-packs unchanged at `5 pack(s), 5 coverage flag(s)` (no new packs in PR 3) plus the new gen-api-dev-adapters codegen output committed (baseline only; CI overwrites on every build per CONTRIBUTING.md pattern).
PR 3: cut branch claude/adev-330-dispatch-and-mobile from origin/main (22c45cd or later). Open PR; link ADEV-330; merge once green.

After PR 3 lands: stack-forge moves to pr-4-starting. PRs 4-7 parallelize. PR 4 (ADEV-334) modern web bulk, PR 5 (ADEV-335) enterprise bulk, PR 6 (ADEV-336) Apple/iOS/Swift, PR 6.5 (ADEV-343 — note the Linear ticket id collision with the ADEV-343 walker-fix commit message; resolved at PM-tool layer), PR 7 (ADEV-337) hosting adapters. PR 8 (ADEV-331) ships the v1.4 release.
